entry.title: Alerts and notifications have been trigger even though the effective date is way passed
entry.content:
Hello team,

Let me explain the situation:

The employees were imported to production System in February 2024

notification configuration was passed in June 2024 

so we triggered a job to collect those employees in the manage alert and notifications ec alerts so they would have their  FUTURE notifications pending to be triggered.

then we triggered EC alerts and not. job selecting January 2024 as the date.

What happened to our surprise is that notifications where the THEN statement in the rule is date plus + 4 days have been triggered also, although that date is way passed, the notification has been triggered anyway.

I was assuming that the alert is only triggered if the job triggers (usually today unless triggered manually at a past day) at the Alert effective day indicated here, and if this date is past, then the alert wouldn´t trigger, but it did

 

 

Is this the standard behavior to launch notifications long after the effective date has already passed?

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - How to transition to Shared Environment - Shared to all users
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We recently added a new option that allows to share an environment to all users. This would ease the transition from public environment to shared environment as explained in a previous blog post.

How it works?

You can create a shared environment as usual. Now in the sharing you have a new option:

After selecting this option, you can decide the rights applicable for all users:

The bare minimum right would be "Monitor". With "Deploy" a Process Automation Developer would be granted to deploy a project in this shared environment.

At the end you will have all the benefits from Shared Environment already mentioned here or here. It's also possible to update an existing shared environment with this option too.

Development Tenant VS. Production Tenant

  • In a "development tenant", you may enable this new shared environment with all rights... except administrate.
  • In a "production tenant", you may restrict this new shared environment with the right "execute" only.

Choice is yours as Process Automation Administrator.

More information about:

  • API Keys - help topic
  • Processes and Workflows within Environment - help topic
  • Manage Destinations - help topic
  • Triggers - help topic
  • Add an API trigger to a project - help topic
  • SAP Note for public environment sunset - https://me.sap.com/notes/3522611

entry.title: Re: Connection issue in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @KarthikKumar31 

I think I saw this error message in the past as well... but long time ago, 1-2 years.

First thing I would do is to check if the Cloud connector can reach your SAP Test system ... means does the icon in the CC appear in green, showing the system is reached.

If not, this is the root cause.

If yes, maybe you check SU53 for your connection user, after testing it again? Can you find any entries there?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Request for Solution: CDS View with Parameters (Key D...
entry.content:
Hello @sridhar123sap 

I'm sorry, but I cannot see the list of CDS views here...

However, if the data changes permanently and the result is controlled by the key date, I would recommend to connect the CDS View directly to SAC as live model and build a story based on this.

If the CDS View is no analytic query, yet (annotation: @Analytics.query:true), then you could create one on top and use this for your live model in SAC.

In this way you can avoid loading daily data/delta and just report live on S4.

Otherwise you can maybe explain what you would like to do with the data in SAP datasphere.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @Juwin 

An action that allows DB insert I don't see coming, seeing as direct DB access in ABAP Cloud is not allowed but I'm also unsure about an action that allows your own commits, since the RAP framework usually takes care of it. I am not one of the makers of RAP, so can't comment on that, if that is possible.

But as for RAP being "counter-intuitive", I kindly disagree and try to explain, if you allow 😊. RAP is different, that's why it may seem counter-intuitive at first - as any language is, at first. These "restrictions"  in RAP are not restrictions, but stability features. RAP ensures that the end-to-end process of whichever behavior is to happen with your object, will be consistent and complete. Can you do a direct DB insert into a z-table in a user exit in classic ABAP? Yes you can. Is that a good thing/stable? No, not at all. You are in the middle of a safe sequence, of something that could, theoretically, still fail to save. But then you already did you direct DB update/insert, thus your data is now inconsistent with what in the end happened with your object. RAP won't allow direct DB inserts or commits during the safe sequence for exactly that reason, and that should never change.

Personally I don't think "additional save" causes any extra hassle. Does it really matter if your code runs directly in an action, unmanaged save, or where-ever you need to have it, or if it is in the additional save? In fact, having additional safe means that RAP isn't lacking in functionality. It simply forces a safe order of things. In this example here, yes it would have been nice to have the APJ creation directly in the action, I totally agree, and I even said "unfortunately we can't" above. But that isn't "RAP's fault": it's keeping my behavior safe and consistent (making sure the behavior first completes, then we can add other updates). In this case, what is missing is that APJ offers an API where I can create an application job that executes with the next commit (or at least let me decide whether I want to force commit or not) and does not always do a direct DB insert.

RAP is really powerful if you get to know it and offers so much out of the box, such as full save and read sequences with full OData query capabilities, including draft handling etc.; you don't need to code any of that. At the same time you can choose to create your own custom query provider. You can choose to create your own unmanaged save. RAP offers both ways; complete framework taking care of the main things or you do your custom codes yourself.

Hope that gave some ideas or insights.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: CPI iFlow getting error: The metadata constraints '[P...
entry.content:
Hello,

the issue was that SAP changed the metadata (from Odata service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV, old doesn't have precision=0) used in CPI iFlow. When we reactivated the iFlow, the metadata updated, and it stopped working. We had to rework all the code (based on new metadata) in our system that sends post requests to iFlow.

Regards,

Patrik

entry.title: Are ABAP Developers Ready for AI with SAP Joule?
entry.content:
I have been actively involved in the SAP ERP domain for nearly 30 years and have witnessed numerous pivotal innovations across SAP’s evolving technology landscape. My ABAP journey began in 1996, when I developed custom code for SAP R/2 HR. As a consultant, I’ve experienced the complete evolution of ABAP—from its early days to the most recent releases—closely following every significant change, enhancement, and breakthrough. Over the past decade, these innovations have had a profound impact on the developer community. However, in my view, no shift has been as revolutionary as the introduction of Joule for Developers and the rise of AI capabilities within the ABAP environment. In this blog, I will share my thoughts with you.

In October 2024, I had the privilege of participating in SAP’s internal workshop series, Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities, through my role as an SAP Mentor. This engagement provided early access to Joule for Developers within ABAP in Eclipse, enabling me to conduct hands-on testing, provide structured evaluations, and contribute feedback ahead of its official launch in February 2025. I use ChatGPT regularly both at work and in my personal life. However, during these workshops, I realized my lack of in-depth knowledge about AI.

I realized that to be future-proof, I must invest in myself, and I was happy that my company entrusted me with developing an AI introduction training program tailored for SAP developers. Additionally, in one of my client engagements, my responsibilities as a solution architect were expanded to include the role of AI advisor. In this role, I had the opportunity to join a dedicated two-week hackathon, where I explored how existing AI capabilities could be leveraged effectively and identified key success factors for deploying AI within complex SAP landscapes. With these opportunities, I'm on my way to closing the AI knowledge gap necessary for success as an SAP Architect and SAP developer, and I want to share some of my experiences.

Joule for Developers

As an SAP enthusiast, I began my journey at the SAP Learning Hub to expand my knowledge and skills. And even though Joule for Developers is relatively new, I found detailed information in the delta course to be certified as an ABAP developer. In this course, it is explained that Joule for Developers supports SAP ABAP developers with AI-based capabilities throughout the development process. This includes scenarios like

  • Predictive code completion: generating ABAP code in the source code editor
  • Joule chat: natural language interaction with an AI-based chat to get support for any development-related questions
  • Explain: explaining development objects or selected lines of code in natural language
  • ABAP Unit: generating and improving unit tests
  • CDS test generation: creating ABAP test classes for CDS entities

The Code Assistant is integrated into the ABAP development tools in Eclipse. It functions as a chatbot that understands complex development objects within ABAP Cloud, allowing developers to ask interactive, natural-language questions and provides real-time answers.

And with the ABAP AI SDK in ABAP Cloud, the developer has even the possible to develop custom generative AI features directly within your business applications. But this capability is currently (June 2025) only possible when your application is running on an ABAP Cloud environment (such as SAP BTP ABAP Environment or ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud). You need also access to SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub, which you only get when your company has an SAP BTP subscription for SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and Generative AI Hub.

For many developers, the journey with Joule for Developers has remained out of reach due to high costs of the BTP services and limited availability within their company SAP environments—but that’s about to change. With the September 2025 release of S/4HANA, AI capabilities will become available for both on-premise and private cloud deployments. SAP also plans to extend compatibility to the 2023 S/4HANA releases. This means we’re just a few months away from a major opportunity with Joule and the broader AI potential SAP is unlocking for developers.

However, as Joule becomes accessible to a wider group of developers, this shift also raises important questions:

Are ABAP developers prepared to leverage the AI capabilities of SAP Joule for Developers entirely?
Are we, as a developer community, truly ready for this transformation?
If not, should we not seek additional guidance and support to navigate this new chapter successfully?

These are critical questions because the answers will ultimately determine the success of Joule for Developers. To explore the answers to these questions, I decided to dive into hands-on research and practical testing.

Research and practical testing

During the development of the AI training, I frequently used ChatGPT to find answers to my questions. Because I also had access to Joule for Developers on an embedded cloud environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, I could check my thoughts and findings directly in Joule for Developers.

I began by asking some general questions about Joule, directing them to both ChatGPT and the Joule platform. Interestingly, both AI tools provided similar responses. SAP Joule is specifically tailored to understand ABAP, SAP data models, and business context, making it more suitable for SAP-centric development. In contrast, tools like OpenAI's ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot offer more general-purpose coding assistance across a wide range of programming languages and platforms.

All the AI tools recommended using SAP Joule as an assistant for ABAP development. However, as a seasoned ABAP developer—with a few gray hairs but an ABAP Cloud certificate—I need more than just a recommendation. I want to be convinced. So, I decided to put both Joule and ChatGPT to the test by asking them to create a function module using the following prompt:

Prompting for an ABAP function

Both tools provided similar ABAP code for the function module. However, Joule went a step further by also offering a step-by-step guide on how to create the function module within an SAP ERP system.

Response from Joule

However, here’s the catch: neither response enables me to run the function module in my S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system. The provided code doesn’t align with clean core principles and utilizes unreleased artifacts. Moreover, Joule’s step-by-step guide is essentially useless in this context, as I’m not allowed to use SAP GUI or traditional transaction codes in a public cloud environment.

 

As an ABAP developer—especially one without a background in AI—I found this experience frustrating. From an SAP-specific AI assistant, I expect valid ABAP code tailored to my system landscape, along with a step-by-step guide that reflects the actual constraints and best practices of S/4HANA Cloud.

Naturally, I tried to solve the issue by asking follow-up questions, but I continued to struggle with incomplete or incorrect answers. It wasn’t until I deepened my understanding of generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and prompt engineering that I began to see why the AI tools responded the way they did. In the world of AI, these issues are referred to as bias and hallucinations. In other words, the problem wasn’t an error in the AI tools themselves—it stemmed from my initial lack of understanding.

It also reminds me of a key insight I came across while researching for the training I developed. In the paper 'On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?' by Emily M. Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell, the authors emphasize — in my own words

GPTs are trained to generate human-like text, not necessarily accurate text.

I found this to be especially true, notably when someone lacks a solid understanding of how generative AI and large language models work. And this so true for the question I asked.

Both models were trained on vast amounts of ABAP code and SAP documentation, learning to identify and reproduce common patterns. So, while Joule was designed to generate ABAP code and step-by-step guides, it wasn't necessarily generating correct or system-appropriate solutions. It was doing exactly what it was trained to do: generating content that looks plausible but is not guaranteed to be valid in a specific context, such as S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

Prompt Engineering

Several techniques—collectively known as AI grounding—can be applied to overcome incorrect answers caused by bias and hallucinations. Some of these, such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), are the responsibility of the AI vendor—in this case, SAP, the provider of the Joule assistant. SAP has already made considerable progress in applying these methods to improve the quality and reliability of Joule's responses.

However, the user of AI tools often has the most significant influence on the accuracy of AI-generated answers—in our case, the ABAP developer. By using effective prompt techniques, selecting the proper AI tool settings, and adhering to established best practices for prompting, developers can significantly enhance the quality and relevance of the answers they receive. Prompting well has become a dedicated discipline, leading to the creation of a new job: the Prompt Engineer. This means that the ABAP developer should learn a new skill, prompt engineering, at least to become a successful developer with SAP Joule.

If I try the same prompt question as before, add context, and follow the zero-shot chain-of-thought (COT) task step-by-step, a widely used reasoning technique in prompt engineering, the Joule result improves significantly.

Zero-shot COT promptResponse from Joule

In the response, Joule provides a step-by-step guide that aligns well with my ABAP development environment in Eclipse. It clearly outlines what I need to do and suggests creating a class rather than using a function module—an approach more typical in an ABAP Cloud environment. It also highlights that direct access to SAP tables and unreleased APIs is not permitted, which aligns with ABAP's core principles of cleanliness.

The provided code skeleton matches what I would expect in an ABAP Cloud setup. However, it falls short in one crucial aspect: it doesn't specify the correct ABAP CDS view to use.

As an ABAP developer, I’d naturally ask follow-up questions like:

What is the CDS view for customer information?

Which released CDS views are available for customers?

Response from Joule

But the answers are often unhelpful, forcing me to search and experiment on my own, which hurts productivity.

A skilled prompt engineer, however, would recognize this approach as weak. Instead, they would refine the prompt using additional context and prompt engineering techniques, iterating multiple times until the correct result is achieved. He will likely develop a prompt like this. In many cases, these tailored prompts can also serve as reusable templates for similar scenarios in the future.

Optimized prompt

Response of Joule

This response reflects the level of support I expect from a SAP AI assistant—providing clear guidance and ready-to-run code.

Conclusion

Based on my research, I can conclude that ABAP developers need at least basic prompt engineering skills—and ideally, a broader understanding of general AI concepts—to effectively utilize SAP Joule for Developers and fully leverage its AI capabilities. Currently, most ABAP developers lack these skills, which limits their ability to derive the maximum value from the tool.

In my opinion, the developer community is not yet fully prepared for the AI transformation, primarily due to a lack of foundational knowledge in AI. I was fortunate to be in a position that allowed me to conduct this research and enhance my understanding of AI while developing AI training for our company. However, this is not the case for most ABAP developers.

To ensure success, SAP needs to provide more structured guidance and support. At the same time, companies must invest in upskilling their developers to capitalize on the opportunities offered by SAP Joule fully.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe 

Disclaimer:
SAP Joule is an AI tool, and the results of prompts may vary over time or when repeated. Thus, the responses Joule provides can differ from those shown in this blog.

entry.title: Re: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hi Ajit,

Thank you for your assistance. Unfortunately, the issue persists.

Upon releasing the wave, two warehouse orders (WOs) are generated. When I confirm the first WO, the Handling Unit (HU) appears in the intermediate bin. From there, another WO is created to place the product into the staging area. Subsequently, upon confirming the second WO, it also appears in the intermediate bin, triggering the creation of yet another WO to move this HU to the staging area.

What I require is for the picker handling the second WO to also collect the first two products from the intermediate bin, consolidate them into a single HU, and place it directly into the staging area.

entry.title: Re: Work Around for Org Replication error –Manager is not valid in the Period
entry.content:
@ramya_26 you probably found the solution already, but you can search in SPRO "Restrict Employee Master Data Replication to Certain Countries" (under "Integration with SuccessFactors Employee Central" => "Other Integration Settings" => "Replicating Data from Employee Central to SAP ERP" => "Country Filtering") there you list all the countries that are in scope. So let's say, if you have Germany, but not Austria, the system will not try to replicate or assign an employee to an Austrian manager. 

Obviously if you have a structure with mixed countries, this will create "holes" in your SAP OM structure. This can be an issue if you have workflow or approval processes on S/4 side.

entry.title: Configuration of certificates and e-mails for Milestone Awards
entry.content:
Milestones Awards work slightly different comparing to Spot Awards:

  • There is no certificate created under Rewards & Recognitions tab
  • There are no e-mails triggered to a nominee/his or her manager.

This was frequently the reason, why my customer didn´t want to proceed with this piece of Comp&Ben. For one customer we had to create a workaround to meet above stated requirements. In this blog I would like to share tips and tricks so you can save the time re-inventing the wheel.

Step 1 – Create a document generation group with a category "Workflow".

This is essential to be able to send an e-mail.

 

Step 2 – Define Document template.

Here we will configure the layout of an e-mail notification or a certificate. Unfortunately, the sent out e-mail can´t be saved in the system.

The first image is showing the certificate template which will be sent as an e-mail. This can be configured via the UI editor as well as directly in HTML.

The second image is showing e-mail template.

Step 3 – Once the template has been created and linked to a group, we need to map placeholders to respective data.

The challenges we’ve encountered here were related to the name of an employee and number of years. Number of years in our case determine when employees receive a milestone award. This data cannot be taken as a reference data from the program itself and should come from a business rule.

Step 4 – Create respective business rules.

Step 5 – Define a workflow to trigger your e-mails

Step 6 – Assign this workflow to a business rule, which will be then mapped to a non-recurring pay component.

Step 7 – Map your general business rule to non-recurring pay component

To test the proposed solution above, please keep in mind the following limitations:

  • Test users should have a unique e-mail. As soon as the system encounters that e-mail address repeats, no e-mail will be sent out.
  • There is a possibility to trigger respective eligibility job for milestone awards only once a day manually, so prepare your test data carefully before you trigger the scheduled job.
  • The scheduled job itself is running once a day.
  • There is no way to show to manager milestone awards of his/her team under Awards History.

If you have any adjustments or enhancement to this article, please write them as a comment.

 

Kind regards,

Anna

entry.title: Re: How do I know if SAP Cash Management is activated?
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, you can check whether SAP Cash Management is activated and see which scope items are related to it by following these steps:

 

How to check if SAP Cash Management is activated :

1. Go to “Manage Your Solution” App (or “Configure Your Solution” for implementation projects):

    • Navigate to App Finder > search for “Manage Your Solution”.
    • Select the “Set Up Your Solution” tile.

 

2. Search for Scope Items:

    • In the Implementation Activities view, click on the “Scope” tab.
    • Enter “Cash Management” or specific scope item IDs in the search field.
    • Look for relevant scope items (see list below) and check their status (e.g., “Activated”).

 

3. Check the Configuration Environment:

    • In the “Manage Your Solution” app, under Application Area: Finance > Sub Application Area: Cash and Liquidity Management, check if related configuration steps are available.
    • If they are grayed out, Cash Management may not be activated.

 

4. Use Apps Related to Cash Management:

  • Cash Position Details
  • Liquidity Forecast
  • Manage Bank Accounts
    • Try opening apps like:
    • If these are unavailable or throw errors, it likely means the scope is not active.

Scope items related to SAP Cash Management

Here are the main scope items associated with SAP Cash Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition:

Scope Item

Name

Description

1SG

Cash Management

Core cash management setup including cash position and liquidity forecast

J78

Bank Relationship Management

Master data for bank accounts, signatories, overdraft limits

BFA

Advanced Cash Operations

Includes grouping, pooling, bank fees, and intercompany netting

1KV

Liquidity Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud

Integrates cash planning with SAC

22C

Intercompany Netting

Facilitates automated netting of payables and receivables across group companies

3FF

In-House Cash

For large enterprises managing centralized payments and internal bank accounts

1J2

Integration with Bank Communication Management (BCM)

Payment control and approval workflows

 

I hope those elements will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Session 1 Recap & Highlights– Data Inspired – Successful concepts for data-driven organizations
entry.content:
Our first speaker Helmut Grimm talked about the concept of Flywheel that was introduced as part of Christian Klein’s keynote at SAP Sapphire 2025. Grimm introduced SAP's transformative flywheel concept, positioning data as the critical center of organizational innovation. He emphasized that in the age of AI, the interplay between applications, data, and artificial intelligence has become more crucial than ever. The flywheel metaphor illustrates how data acts as the fundamental engine driving business acceleration, requiring a seamless integration of technological components.

 Helmut emphasized that today,  Data sits at the center of organizational innovation and best of suite approach enables seamless interplay between apps, data, and AI that is extremely critical. Helmut also introduced the newly launched SAP Architecture Center as a strategic resource for data integration and must visit for every EA.

Our next speaker, Dr. Sebastian Wernicke laid the vision of going from Data-Driven to Data-Inspired. Sebastian challenged traditional data utilization approaches, introducing the revolutionary concept of being "data-inspired" rather than merely "data-driven". He argued that most organizations struggle not with data collection, but with transforming data insights into meaningful organizational change.

These challenges include Persistent skills gaps in driving value from data, Unclear business cases for data initiatives, insufficient management attention to data strategies, difficulty in breaking down organizational silos and Limited ability to transform data insights into actionable change within and outside the organization.

 

While there are data challenges in every org, it is an great opportunity for Data Architects and Enterprise Architects to change things by driving – holistic organizational learning and through continuous conversation with senior executives about their data initiatives.

Sebastian also talk about how Enterprise Architects can drive towards being Data Inspired through these 4 element that are usually in their sphere of influence:

  1. Radical Data Integrity -  The foundational approach to building trustworthy data ecosystems. Enterprise architects must develop robust governance frameworks that ensure data quality, create transparent lineage, and implement metadata management strategies. This involves not just technological solutions, but also developing organizational cultures that prioritize data accuracy and reliability.
  2. Connected Data Ecosystems bringing a holistic approach to breaking down traditional organizational silos. Enterprise architects must design integrated platforms that facilitate seamless data sharing across different functional areas. This involves creating technical infrastructures and collaborative mechanisms that encourage cross-functional communication and insight generation.
  3. Strategic Incentive Structures i.e. reimagining of organizational reward systems. By aligning incentives with data-sharing and insight generation, enterprise architects can create cultural mechanisms that inherently value data-driven decision-making. This goes beyond traditional performance metrics, embedding a data-inspired mindset into the core of organizational behavior.
  4. Decision Architecture Design as a critical capability for modern enterprise architects. This involves developing flexible frameworks that integrate data insights directly into strategic decision-making processes. The goal is to create organizational systems that support continuous learning, adaptability, and strategic responsiveness.

 The inaugural webinar of the series was a huge success with the participants as the speakers presented a compelling narrative that goes far beyond traditional data management, positioning data as a strategic asset capable of driving fundamental organizational change. As SAP customers start their Business Data Cloud journey, the speakers acknowledged that it is not about accumulating more data or implementing sophisticated technologies, but about fundamentally reimagining how organizations perceive, utilize, and derive value from their data ecosystems. Enterprise architects stand at the forefront of this transformation, serving as critical architects of not just technological infrastructure, but of organizational change and adaptability.

You can check out Sebastian's book here: https://www.amazon.com/Data-inspired-author/dp/3800671298

entry.title: Session 2 Recap & Highlights– Unleash your Data and AI with Irfan Khan and Timo Elliott
entry.content:
The session started with Irfan doing a quick round up of SAP BDC starting with the Why? behind the launch of Business Data Cloud. Irfan pointed a fundamental problem: businesses spend approximately 82% of their time on data curation & manipulation rather than business transformation. Here, SAP Business Data Cloud emerges as a revolutionary solution, designed to flip this paradigm by providing a modern Lake House architecture that harmonizes data across SAP and non-SAP systems.

Irfan highlighted that the key breakthrough of BDC is its approach to data products. By the end of 2024, SAP plans to offer around 500 data products, each representing intricate details of various business applications. The push logic mechanism allows seamless data ingestion from core ERP systems, creating a more efficient and intelligent data management ecosystem.

During the Q&A session, Timo Elliott skillfully extracted critical insights from Khan. One pivotal discussion revolved around data quality - a perennial challenge in enterprise computing to which Irfan candidly acknowledged that while AI can help improve data matching and cleanup, organizations must still institutionalize robust data governance practices.

Irfan talked about importance of knowledge graphs - a technology that enables more nuanced, context-aware data interactions. By using vertices and edges to identify patterns and similarities, knowledge graphs can answer complex, open-ended business questions that traditional SQL interfaces cannot.  Irfan also talked about SAP BDC being an open platform designed to work with various data sources and technologies. The partnership with Databricks, for instance, demonstrates SAP's commitment to providing flexible, interoperable solutions.

 Here are some key highlights from the Q&A session between Timo Elliott and Irfan Khan:

  1. Timo: What are the key technologies in Business Data Cloud that you're most proud of?

Irfan's highlighted the foundation service built on a modern Lake House architecture, with a unique push logic for data ingestion. He was particularly excited about creating a data product hierarchy that has been open-sourced on GitHub, allowing customers and partners to build their own data products already.

  1. Timo: What makes intelligent applications different from previous analytics applications?

Irfan's emphasized 3 key differences:

  • The integration of agents as an integral part of the applications
  • Ensuring that these applications are grounded in reliable, high-quality data with preserved business context
  • The ability to create more functionally rich applications that can curate data from both SAP and non-SAP sources
  1. Timo: How do customers with existing SAP data platforms like BW or HANA transition to Business Data Cloud?

Irfan explained that  customers have multiple options:

  • They can semantically onboard their existing data products into BDC
  • For BW systems, this could mean modernizing by bringing in data and building AI/ML capabilities on top of curated data products
  • There's flexibility to run existing systems in parallel with BDC
  1. Timo: How is SAP addressing data quality, a long-standing challenge?

Irfan acknowledged that data quality remains a complex issue. While AI can help with tasks like address matching, organizations must still:

  • Institutionalize data governance
  • Use proper master data management tools
  • Ensure data quality before implementation projects
  • Leverage BDC's capabilities to provide curated, semantically enriched data

As the webcast drew to a close, the conversation between Timo Elliott and Irfan Khan crystallized a powerful message for enterprise architects: we are standing at the precipice of a transformative era in Data and Artificial Intelligence. SAP Business Data Cloud is not just a technological solution, but a strategic capability that empowers organizations to transcend traditional data limitations. By harmonizing SAP and non-SAP data, embedding intelligent agents, and providing a robust, flexible architecture, SAP is offering more than a product – it's providing a vision of how businesses can truly unlock the potential of their data. For enterprise architects, this represents an unprecedented opportunity to move from being mere technology managers to becoming strategic innovators who can reshape how organizations understand, interact with, and derive value from their most critical asset: their data. The future is not about managing data; it's about orchestrating intelligence. To all the Enterprise Architects attending the session, the message was clear: the future belongs to those who can effectively harmonize data, leverage AI, and create intelligent, context-aware systems.

Note: Slides from the session and the recording would be only available to the registered attendees. So if you have not registered, please register now using the link below: https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

entry.title: Re: Create new DME format under Format Tree UMS1 - S4Hana...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

You're absolutely right: in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the classic DMEE transaction used in on-premise systems to create or edit DMEE format trees (such as UMS1) is not available.

This means the process of modifying or creating new format trees like you would in ECC or S/4HANA On-Premise is restricted in the cloud version.

Here’s a breakdown of your options and guidance specific to UMS1 and the Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases app in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition :

 

Why you can't create or modify DMEE trees in Public Cloud

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • All DMEE trees are delivered by SAP and are not customer-editable.
  • The concept of extensibility does not extend to DMEE tree editing.
  • SAP controls and maintains these format trees to ensure legal compliance (especially for statutory reports like VAT returns, SAF-T, etc.).

 

What you can do instead

1. Use the « Map Format Data for Payments » App

You can copy from an existing DMEE format delivered by SAP and enter in the « Edit » mode to create / modify node directly

 

2. Raise a Feature Request or SAP Incident

If you have a specific requirement for a custom format or minor variation of UMS1, you can:

  • Raise a SAP Influence Request for a new format version.
  • Open a SAP Support Incident and ask if there's a supported workaround, or if SAP can expose additional BAdIs for your use case.

 

3. Post-Processing the Output Externally

If your requirement is to:

  • Slightly adapt the structure
  • Add headers, tags, or adjust fields

…then the most practical solution is to:

  • Generate the standard UMS1 XML
  • Use middleware (e.g., SAP BTP Integration Suite, CPI) to transform the output externally to your desired format.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

 

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: If I run the automation in test mode of BPA, will it...
entry.content:
Hi Aye Chan,

It will not be counted as an Attended or Unattended mode connection.

You will be charged as an Advanced User.

You can check the costing using the following link: SAP Estimator (just change the commercial model as per your subscription).

  • If not, when will the connection be counted to charge a cost and how many connections will be needed for my scenario?

For this question, when you deploy your project, schedule it, and run it in unattended mode, it will be charged as an unattended connection.

For attended mode, you will need to add the Automation Launcher to your project and include the particular process in it. After deploying that process, you will see your project in the Desktop Agent under the Project tab. When you run that project, you will be charged as an attended connection.

 

Regards,

Manish

entry.title: Unlocking the Future of Digital Marketing: How AI-Driven Voice Search Is Revolutionizing Business
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of digital marketing, few trends have captured the imagination and driven innovation quite like voice search. As we navigate through 2025, it's clear that voice search is no longer just a novelty, it's a full-fledged revolution. With AI at the helm, voice search is transforming how we interact with content, and businesses are racing to adapt. To leverage this trend effectively, marketers can benefit from enrolling in SEO Certificate Programs, which provide comprehensive insights into optimizing for voice search. Additionally, for those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can offer specialized knowledge on how to tailor content for local voice search queries. For those interested in learning more about SEO in the context of voice search, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can provide valuable hands-on experience.

Introduction to Voice Search

Voice search has come a long way since its inception. From its early days as a feature on smartphones to its current ubiquity in smart speakers and virtual assistants, voice search has evolved significantly. Today, it's projected that voice search will account for a substantial portion of all searches, with over 1 billion voice searches conducted each month. This dramatic shift is driven by the increasing sophistication of voice assistants like Google Assistant, Apple’s Siri, and Amazon’s Alexa, along with the growing adoption of smart speakers and smartphones. By the end of 2025, voice search is expected to account for over 50% of all searches, marking a significant milestone in its evolution. Marketers looking to capitalize on this trend should consider enrolling in SEO Certificate Programs to gain a deeper understanding of voice search optimization strategies. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help tailor these strategies for local businesses. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai courses can provide essential insights into how to optimize content for voice search.

The Evolution of Voice Search

Early Days to Current Trends

Voice search began as a feature on smartphones, allowing users to perform searches using voice commands. However, with the advent of smart speakers and virtual assistants, voice search has become ubiquitous. Today, voice assistants are integrated into various devices, from smartphones and smart speakers to cars and home appliances. This integration has made voice search a seamless part of daily life, allowing users to access information, control devices, and perform tasks with ease. For those interested in optimizing for voice search, SEO Certificate Programs can provide valuable insights into how to structure content for these devices. Additionally, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses in Mumbai understand how to tailor their content for local voice queries. For those looking to dive deeper into SEO strategies, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical experience in optimizing for voice search.

Impact on Consumer Behavior

Voice search has significantly impacted consumer behavior, particularly in how people interact with content. Voice queries are often more conversational and question-based, which requires businesses to rethink their content strategies. For instance, voice search results tend to come from featured snippets and top-ranking pages, favoring long-form, conversational content. This shift has led to a greater emphasis on creating content that answers questions directly and naturally. Marketers can benefit from SEO Certificate Programs to learn how to craft such content effectively. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses adapt their content strategies for local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai courses can provide detailed guidance on optimizing content for voice search.

Statistical Overview

  • Adoption Rates: The number of voice assistant users in the U.S. is expected to reach 162.7 million by 2025.
  • Usage Patterns: 76% of smart speaker users perform local voice searches at least weekly.
  • Satisfaction Levels: 93% of consumers are satisfied with their voice assistants, indicating high trust and engagement.

To better understand these statistics and how they impact local businesses, SEO Certificate Programs can offer comprehensive insights into voice search trends. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can provide strategies tailored to the local market. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can help marketers analyze and leverage these statistics effectively.

Latest Features, Tools, and Trends

AI Integration in Voice Search

AI is at the heart of voice search's evolution. By leveraging AI, voice assistants can better understand natural language, offer more accurate results, and even predict user intent. This integration has significantly improved the user experience, making voice search more intuitive and efficient. AI-driven voice assistants can now handle complex queries and provide personalized responses, enhancing the overall experience for users. For marketers seeking to optimize for these AI-driven voice assistants, SEO Certificate Programs can provide essential training on AI integration in voice search. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can help businesses understand how AI impacts local voice search strategies. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can offer practical insights into leveraging AI for voice search optimization.

Voice Search Optimization Tools

  • SEO Auditing Tools: Platforms like Ahrefs and SEMrush now include features specifically designed to analyze and improve voice search rankings. These tools help identify gaps in content and provide insights into how to better optimize for voice queries.
  • Content Optimization Tools: Tools like Content Blossom help create content that is more conversational and optimized for voice queries. This includes crafting content that answers questions directly and naturally.
  • Structured Data and Schema Markup: Essential for helping voice assistants understand and feature your content, schema markup has become a crucial element in voice search optimization. By using schema markup, businesses can ensure that their content is highlighted in voice search results.

For marketers looking to utilize these tools effectively, SEO Certificate Programs can provide comprehensive training on how to integrate them into their voice search strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses choose the right tools for local voice search optimization. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer hands-on experience with these tools.

Voice Commerce and Its Impact

Voice commerce is another area where AI-driven voice search is making a significant impact. With voice commerce projected to reach $80 billion annually by 2025, businesses are leveraging AI to enhance the shopping experience. This includes using AI to personalize product recommendations, streamline checkout processes, and improve customer service. Voice commerce allows customers to shop hands-free, making it a convenient option for busy consumers. For those interested in optimizing for voice commerce, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how AI enhances the shopping experience. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses understand how to integrate voice commerce into their local strategies. Furthermore, strong>Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on optimizing for voice commerce.

Advanced Tactics for Success

Conversational Content Creation

One of the most effective strategies for optimizing content for voice search is to create conversational, question-based content. Voice queries are often phrased as questions, so crafting content that answers these questions directly can significantly improve visibility in voice search results. This involves using natural language and focusing on long-form content that provides comprehensive answers. For marketers seeking to create such content, SEO Certificate Programs can provide valuable training on conversational content creation. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai courses can help businesses tailor their content for local voice search queries. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into crafting conversational content.

Local SEO and Voice Search

Local businesses see the greatest impact from voice search optimization. With 58% of consumers using voice to find local information, businesses must ensure their local SEO is robust. This includes optimizing Google My Business listings, using location-specific keywords, and ensuring accurate NAP (Name, Address, Phone Number) consistency across the web. For those in Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses optimize their local SEO strategies for voice search. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide detailed guidance on local SEO optimization. Marketers can also benefit from SEO Certificate Programs to gain a comprehensive understanding of local SEO strategies.

Mobile Optimization

Since the majority of voice searches occur on smartphones, mobile optimization is no longer optional. Ensuring that your website is mobile-friendly, loads quickly, and provides a seamless user experience is crucial for success in voice search. This includes optimizing images, minimizing page load times, and ensuring that all content is easily accessible on mobile devices. For marketers seeking to optimize their websites for mobile, SEO Certificate Programs can provide essential training on mobile optimization techniques. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses ensure their websites are mobile-friendly for local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into mobile optimization strategies.

Storytelling and Community Engagement

Content remains the backbone of any successful SEO strategy, including voice search. By focusing on storytelling and community engagement, businesses can create a deeper connection with their audience. This can be achieved by:

  • Storytelling: Using narratives to make your content more relatable and engaging. This can help increase engagement and brand loyalty.
  • Community Engagement: Encouraging user-generated content and fostering discussions around your brand. This not only builds trust but also provides valuable insights into what your audience is looking for.

For marketers interested in leveraging storytelling and community engagement, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how these strategies enhance voice search visibility. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses integrate storytelling into their local voice search strategies. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on community engagement for voice search optimization.

Influencer Partnerships and User-Generated Content

Influencer partnerships and user-generated content can play a significant role in enhancing voice search visibility. By partnering with influencers who have a strong voice search presence, businesses can tap into their audience and increase brand awareness. User-generated content, meanwhile, provides social proof and can help improve local SEO by showcasing real customer experiences. For marketers seeking to leverage influencer partnerships, SEO Certificate Programs can provide training on how to integrate these partnerships into voice search strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses find influencers with a strong local presence. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into using user-generated content for local voice search optimization.

Measuring Success: Analytics and Insights

Measuring the success of voice search strategies requires a nuanced approach. Traditional metrics like website traffic and engagement metrics are still relevant, but businesses must also track voice-specific metrics such as:

  • Voice Search Conversions: Monitor how many users complete a desired action (e.g., making a purchase or filling out a form) after using voice search.
  • Voice Assistant Engagement: Track how users interact with voice assistants to access your content.

Tools like Google Analytics can be adapted to track these metrics, providing valuable insights into the effectiveness of your voice search strategies. For marketers seeking to measure the success of their voice search efforts, SEO Certificate Programs can offer comprehensive training on analytics and insights. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses set up analytics for local voice search metrics. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on interpreting voice search analytics.

Business Case Study: Domino's Pizza

Dominos Pizza is a prime example of a brand that has successfully leveraged AI-driven voice search strategies. By integrating with voice assistants like Alexa and Google Assistant, Domino's allows customers to order pizzas using voice commands. This not only enhances customer convenience but also drives sales through voice commerce. Domino's faced the challenge of ensuring that their voice ordering system was seamless and user-friendly. They worked closely with AI developers to refine their voice ordering process, ensuring that it could handle a wide range of voice commands and accents. The results were impressive. Domino's saw a significant increase in orders placed through voice assistants, with customers appreciating the convenience and speed of ordering pizzas using voice commands. This strategy not only improved customer satisfaction but also positioned Domino's as a leader in voice commerce. For marketers interested in replicating this success, SEO Certificate Programs can provide insights into how to integrate AI-driven voice search into their strategies. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses understand how to apply similar strategies to local voice commerce. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical guidance on optimizing for voice commerce.

Additional Case Studies

To further illustrate the impact of voice search, let's consider a few more case studies:

  • Amazon: Amazon's Alexa has been instrumental in promoting voice commerce. By allowing users to shop using voice commands, Amazon has seen significant growth in voice-driven sales.
  • Walmart: Walmart has integrated voice shopping into its app, allowing customers to order groceries and other items using voice assistants. This has improved customer convenience and increased sales.

For marketers seeking to learn from these case studies, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how to apply similar strategies to their own businesses. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses analyze these case studies in the context of local voice search. Furthermore, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on how to integrate voice commerce into local strategies.

Future Developments in Voice Search

As voice search continues to evolve, several future developments are worth noting:

  • Advancements in AI: Future advancements in AI will likely improve the accuracy and personalization of voice assistants, making them even more intuitive and user-friendly.
  • New Devices and Integrations: The integration of voice assistants into more devices, such as cars and home appliances, will continue to expand the reach of voice search.
  • Privacy Concerns: As voice search becomes more ubiquitous, privacy concerns will become increasingly important. Businesses will need to ensure that user data is protected while still providing personalized experiences.

For marketers interested in staying ahead of these developments, SEO Certificate Programs can provide comprehensive training on future trends in voice search. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses prepare for these future developments in the local market. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai workshops can offer practical insights into how to adapt to future voice search technologies.

Challenges in Implementing Voice Search Strategies

Voice search offers numerous opportunities, but businesses also face several challenges when implementing voice search strategies:

  • Technical Challenges: Ensuring that voice search systems are technically sound and can handle a wide range of queries and accents is crucial.
  • Content Optimization: Creating content that is optimized for voice search requires a significant shift in content strategy, which can be challenging for some businesses.
  • User Adoption: Encouraging users to adopt voice search can be difficult, especially among older demographics.

Solutions to Common Challenges

  • Collaboration with AI Developers: Working closely with AI developers can help refine voice search systems and ensure they are user-friendly.
  • Content Strategy Overhaul: Businesses should be willing to overhaul their content strategy to focus on conversational, question-based content.
  • User Education: Educating users about the benefits of voice search can encourage adoption and increase usage.

For marketers seeking to overcome these challenges, SEO Certificate Programs can offer insights into how to address technical and content-related issues. In Mumbai, Digital Marketing Training Mumbai can help businesses develop strategies to encourage user adoption of voice search. Additionally, Learn SEO in Mumbai initiatives can provide practical guidance on content strategy overhauls for voice search optimization.

Actionable Tips for Marketers

As you navigate the world of AI-driven voice search, here are some actionable tips to help you succeed:

  1. Optimize for Conversational Queries: Craft content that answers questions directly, using natural language.
  2. Use Structured Data: Ensure your website includes schema markup to help voice assistants understand your content.
  3. Focus on Local SEO: If you're a local business, prioritize optimizing your Google My Business listing and using location-specific keywords.

entry.title: Re: Business Rules (on object, Save) not being triggered...
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe 

In the business rule which you mapped in the event type "OnSave", navigate to the "Details" section and at "Rule Contexts" section can you check the input for "Import"? If it is "no" then while importing this save rule won't be considered. 

To validate the same, try to change the input for "Import" to "yes" then execute the import. Recommended to check this feature in test environment, if it is working as expected then replicate the same in Production.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Gordon,

I have aligned your question with our product manager for the supplier invoice.

We are not 100% clear what you mean with 'spliiting the posting' / 'splitting the ICo supplier invoice'.

From our perspective the system should post one supplier invoice for every supplier invoice being received, means one legal invoice must not be split into 2 or more supplier invoices in the system.
Instead of posting one legal invoice as several supplier invoices you should split the customer invoice instead.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: How to change date format to YYYY-MM-DD Story Report
entry.content:
Hi,

There isn't an easy way to change the date format directly in Story Reports. However, SAP provides a KBA that explains how to manually convert the date format:
2986093 - How to manually change the Date Format for a date field in Story report - SAP for Me

I’d also like to share my formula for this, including how to return 0 if the month or day  have one digits.

CONCAT(
TOTEXT(YEAR([FIELDNAME])),
CONCAT("-",
CONCAT(
RIGHT(CONCAT("0", TOTEXT(MONTH([FIELDNAME]))), 2),
CONCAT("-",
RIGHT(CONCAT("0", TOTEXT(DAY([FIELDNAME]))), 2)
)
)
)
)

entry.title: Re: Strategy Group 52 in SAP IBP (Planning w/o Final Asse...
entry.content:
Custom development:

  • Use the new oDATA feature of OBP to download pegging results.
  • Write a custom SE38 report that sets the conversion indicator for planned orders based on your oDATA extraction of planned order pegging.
  • Use periodic RTI Outbound integration for planned orders.
  • Use an external scheduler and make sure that the jobs for:
  1. the OBP run,
  2. the oData Download
  3. Job Based RTI Outbound of planned orders
  4. and the custom S/4 report

are scheduled one after the other without a time gap in between.


See also: Extracting Detailed Pegging Data for External Systems | SAP Help Portal / Step-by-Step Guide: Extracting Pegging Data with t... - SAP Community.

entry.title: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Após muita confusão e dúvidas sobre a partir de qual data as NF-e emitidas em homologação sem as tags da Reforma Tributária serão rejeitadas, o Governo se pronunciou e publicou a atualização da NT 2025.002 v 1.10, destacando a data de 06/10/2025 para rejeição dos documentos sem as tags da reforma tributária no ambiente de homologação.
Essa atualização trará um alívio nos projetos, uma vez que todos estavam muito preocupados com a temática, ainda mais aqueles que estão implementando - além da reforma - o SAP DRC (substituto do SAP GRC NFe), uma vez que ambos os temas começam a se misturar e a SAP está lançando Notas para adequar o GRC à Reforma Tributária.

Para Produção, ainda está mantida a data de 01/01/2026.

O tempo é curto, os desafios são muitos e o governo continua firme (até o momento) sobre as datas das mudanças da Reforma.

A NT 2025.002 v 1.10, na íntegra, está disponível em <https://lnkd.in/d_B6KdKi>

entry.title: Re: need help connecting Salesforce to SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
To connect your Salesforce Connected App to SAP Build Apps, follow these steps:

  1. Set Up OAuth in Salesforce – Ensure your connected app has OAuth enabled with the correct scopes.

  2. Configure SAP BTP Destination – Verify authentication settings and API endpoints in SAP BTP.

  3. Use REST API Correctly – Check if your API calls include the right headers, authentication tokens, and endpoint URLs.

  4. Test with Postman – Validate API connectivity outside SAP Build Apps to identify potential issues.
